title: rubus arduennensis subsp. moestus
intro: Rubus arduennensis subsp. moestus is a sub-species of the raspberry plant native to the Ardennes region in Europe. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits and leaves that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The leaves and roots of Rubus arduennensis subsp. moestus contain anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, and can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
